evidence: The paper states: "The Ranger package uses a method introduced by Janitza et al 29 to calculate feature importance. This method is built on a revised permutation variable importance inspired by cross-validation procedures." Janitza et al (2018) describes permutation-based importance, not impurity importance. While the paper does interpret feature rankings (e.g., "In case 3, the SD of raw accelerometer data in the Y-axis is the most deciding predictor"), this analysis is secondary and not central to the main conclusions about model accuracy (89-92.9% for Case 3). No significance testing is applied to the importance measures, and the findings are not corroborated by other methods.
